conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now I can help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child, Alex, who is 14 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 14 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

**Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, there are specific circumstances where you may still be able to claim Child Benefit.

**Which country are you currently living in?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm living in Spain at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Living in Spain may affect your eligibility for UK Child Benefit.

To help determine if you can still claim Child Benefit while living in Spain, I need to ask:

**Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, and I'm not a Crown servant either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. 

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarize your situation:
- You have one child, Alex, who is 14 years old
- You are currently living in Spain
- You do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- You are not a Crown servant

According to the UK Child Benefit rules, you normally need to live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. The exceptions to this rule are if you're living in certain countries and you're a Crown servant (such as a diplomat or member of the armed forces), or if you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with additional qualifying conditions.

Since you're living in Spain and don't meet any of the exceptions that would allow you to claim Child Benefit while living abroad, unfortunately you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

**The reason:** While Alex meets the age requirement at 14 years old, the main issue is that you're living in Spain. To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to live in the UK. The only exceptions for claiming while living abroad are if you're a Crown servant or if you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with additional conditions). Since you don't meet any of these exceptions, you cannot claim Child Benefit while living in Spain.

If your circumstances change in the future - for example, if you move back to the UK - you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time. If you have any other questions, please don't hesitate to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child is fostered but the council is not paying for accommodation or maintenance
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 14 years old, which meets the age requirement (under 16). However, the claimant is living in Spain and does not meet any of the exceptions that allow claiming Child Benefit while living abroad. The claimant is not a Crown servant and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. According to the eligibility rules, you normally must live in the UK to qualify for Child Benefit. Since the claimant does not live in the UK and does not qualify under any exception for living abroad, they are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (aged 14), but is currently living in Spain without settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme and is not a Crown servant. The fundamental eligibility requirement is that you must live in the UK to claim Child Benefit, with limited exceptions for those living abroad. As the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions, they are not eligible for Child Benefit.
